commit.message:
feat: smart priority-based file selection for large codebases

Fixes the hard 50-file limit that failed on projects with 22,423+ files.

Changes:
- codeAnalyzer.ts: scoreFileByImportance() prioritizes pages/routes/views
  directories (score 100) then known entry points and page-named files,
  then components, before generic files — so the 400-file budget is spent
  on the files that matter most for writing user-facing tests
- codeAnalyzer.ts: readFileSync moved inside try/catch — prevents a single
  unreadable/locked file from crashing the entire analysis run
- codeAnalyzer.ts: ADDITIONAL_EXCLUDE_PATTERNS filters out stories, .d.ts,
  .min.js, vendor, mocks, fixtures, storybook, migrations
- generate.ts: DEFAULT_MAX_COMPONENT_COUNT 50 -> 400
- testPlanner.ts: maxFileCount 50 -> 200
- config.ts: globalExcludePatterns expanded with 10 additional noise filters

commit.message:
feat: application layer — contracts, LLM gateway, services (#61)

* feat: application layer — contracts, LLM gateway, services

Application layer (agents/src/application/) — the shared core that CLI,
Python MCP server, and future Web API all wrap.

Components:
- contracts/agents.py — Pydantic request/response models for all 7 agents,
  pipeline, and digest. Modeled around actual agent capabilities.
- llm/types.py — LLMProvider protocol, LLMMessage, LLMResponse
- llm/gateway.py — LLMGateway with synthesize(), create_gateway() factory
- llm/litellm_provider.py — LiteLLM-based multi-provider implementation
- llm/mock.py — MockProvider (testing) + SkipProvider (MCP path)
- services/agent_service.py — maps typed contracts to agent run() calls
- services/pipeline_service.py — wraps orchestrator with task_id support
- services/digest_service.py — wraps research pipeline
- config.py — AppConfig via pydantic-settings (env vars)

Orchestrator fix:
- Added task_id to AgentTask and AgentResult
- Pipeline now keys by task.id (not agent_name), enabling same agent
  to run multiple times with different task IDs

Documentation updates:
- architecture.md — reflects implemented app layer, task_id, phasing table
- agents.md — orchestration, application services, gateway status
- copilot-instructions.md — updated tech stack versions

Dependencies added:
- pydantic >=2.11
- pydantic-settings >=2.9
- litellm >=1.72

Tests: 52 new tests (274 total, 5 skipped)

Closes #49

commit.message:
feat: smart priority-based file selection in code analyzer

- Add scoreFileByImportance() function that scores files 100-10 based
  on directory name (pages/routes/views/screens/app), exact component
  name (Login, Dashboard, Cart, etc.), filename suffix (Page, Screen,
  Form, Modal), and path depth
- Add calculatePathDepth() tiebreaker that prefers shallower files
  within the same score tier
- Replace naive slice(0, maxFileCount) with priority-sorted selection
  so the most valuable files are always analyzed when the limit applies
- Add ADDITIONAL_EXCLUDE_PATTERNS to discoverSourceFiles: stories,
  .d.ts stubs, .min.js bundles, .next/build/out/vendor/.turbo/
  coverage/__mocks__/fixtures/mocks/migrations/storybook-static
- Fix parseSourceFile crash: move readFileSync inside try/catch so
  unreadable or locked files produce a warning instead of aborting run
- Raise DEFAULT_MAX_COMPONENT_COUNT from 50 to 400 in generate.ts
- Raise maxFileCount from 50 to 200 in testPlanner.ts
- Expand globalExcludePatterns in config.ts with the same new patterns
- Update codeAnalyzer.spec.ts fixture base directory to avoid matching
  the new fixtures glob exclude pattern
- Change truncation log from logWarning to logInfo with updated message
- Extract AST traversal into extractInteractiveElementsAndImports()
  helper to keep parseSourceFile under 40 lines

commit.message:
Add automatic `flyway` label for PRs touching Flyway migrations (#1280)

### **Behov / Bakgrunn**

PRs som endrer Flyway-migreringer under
`migreringer/src/main/resources/db/postgres/` ble ikke automatisk
merket, noe som gjorde det vanskelig å raskt identifisere
databaseendringer i PR-oversikten.

### **Løsning**

- Legger til `.github/labeler.yml` med glob-regel som trigges på
endringer i `migreringer/src/main/resources/db/postgres/**`
- Legger til `.github/workflows/labeler.yml` som kjører
`actions/labeler@v6` på alle PRs og setter `flyway`-labelen automatisk

Forutsetter at `flyway`-labelen er opprettet manuelt i repoet.

### **Andre endringer**

Ingen.

### **Skjermbilder** (hvis relevant)

N/A

feat: ICL (In-Context Learning) mode for voice cloning (#32) (#33)

* feat: add ref_text ICL mode support for voice cloning (#32)

Implement In-Context Learning (ICL) mode for higher-quality voice cloning
when both reference text and reference audio are provided.

Changes:
- New SpeechTokenizer.cs: ONNX wrapper for tokenizer12hz_encode.onnx,
  encodes raw 24kHz audio into codec codes [1, T, 16]
- LanguageModel.cs: New GenerateWithSpeakerEmbeddingAndRefText() method,
  BuildPrefillEmbedding inserts ICL embeddings (ref text + ref audio codes)
  between codec prefix and target text in prefill sequence
- VoiceClonePipeline.cs: New SynthesizeAsync overload with refText param,
  lazy-loaded SpeechTokenizer, backward-compatible API
- VoiceCloningDownloader.cs: Added tokenizer12hz_encode.onnx to downloads
- MelSpectrogram.cs: Made ReadWav/Resample internal for code reuse

All 245 existing tests pass. Fully backward compatible.

commit.message:
fix(web): resolve CSRF origin mismatch on raw Vercel deployment URLs

Root cause: CSRF validation compared browser Origin header against the
canonical production URL (from NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL). When users landed
on raw deployment URLs (trading-<hash>.vercel.app), Origin didn't match
the canonical host, causing 403 rejections on all mutating requests.

Fix:
- csrf.ts: use OWASP target-origin matching — derive expected origin
  from request.url instead of the canonical env var
- proxy.ts: add production canonical host redirect (308) for page
  GET/HEAD requests on non-canonical hosts
- url.ts: add getRequestOrigin(request), getCanonicalHost(), and
  getCanonicalSiteUrl alias to separate SEO from runtime origin concerns

Security model:
- CSRF still blocks cross-origin attacks (evil.com Origin != request host)
- Production pages redirect to canonical host (SEO + UX normalization)
- Preview deployments work on their own hostnames (no redirect)
- API routes are never redirected (CSRF handles them via origin matching)

Tests: 22 CSRF tests (6 new multi-host), 27 URL tests (8 new),
6 new proxy canonical redirect tests. All 1143 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(emitter): cover emit_extension_files dest.parent() None branch

Add test `emit_extension_files_absolute_path_dest_has_no_parent` to cover
the False branch of `if let Some(parent) = dest.parent()` in
`emit_extension_files`. When `artifact.files` contains an absolute path
(e.g. `PathBuf::from("/")`), Rust's `Path::join` replaces the base,
so `ext_dir.join("/") == PathBuf::from("/")`, and `"/".parent()`
is `None`, meaning the `create_dir_all` guard is skipped.

Before: 95.32% branch coverage (98 missed)
After:  95.37% branch coverage (97 missed)

commit.message:
fix: wrap optional `age` in guard block to fix optional-nested test (#133)

* fix: wrap optional `age` property in guard block in optional-nested template

The `age` property is marked as `optional` in the Concerto model but was
used directly as `{{age}}` without an `{{#optional}}` guard. After PR #53
introduced stricter compile-time checks for optional properties, this
causes the test to fail with: "Optional properties used without guards: age"

Wraps the bare `{{age}}` reference in `{{#optional age}}{{this}}{{/optional}}`
to align with the new validation rules.

Fixes #112
